refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all relevant stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 1.0 s latency.
FR2: Update and distribute the worksite model to all relevant modules within 1.0 s latency.
FR3: Ensure data integrity and consistency during update and distribution.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model management and update engine.
DP3: High-speed, reliable communication interfaces for data distribution.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline; DP2: Centralized worksite model management and update engine; DP3: High-speed, reliable communication interfaces for data distribution.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between DP1 and DP2
Communication interface dependency between DP2 and DP3
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but maintain clear separation of concerns, minimizing tight coupling and enabling modular updates.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency and reliability meet system requirements.
Risk of data loss or delay due to network congestion or hardware failure.
Assumes telemetry data formats are standardized and consistent.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and telemetry data quality continuously; consider fallback mechanisms for degraded network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry ingestion, worksite model management, and data distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data communication between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the core system managing worksite updates; low diffusion risk due to centralized update logic.
Limited containment; primarily data source and consumer; moderate diffusion risk if update logic is duplicated.
Low containment; mainly consumes updated data; higher diffusion risk if update logic is spread here.
Minimal containment; only receives safety alerts; high diffusion risk if update logic is duplicated.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite model update logic within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports timeliness and safety requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution if processing load is high.
Dependency on network link reliability for timely updates.
Risk of single point of failure if NGHE Solution is not sufficiently redundant.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement redundancy and load balancing within NGHE Solution; monitor network health; avoid duplicating update logic in other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to centralized update logic in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements, data integrity checks, and interface testing at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider specifying fallback mechanisms for network failures.
Define monitoring and alerting strategies for update latency breaches.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet the high safety criticality requirements with clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 1.0 s latency.
FR2: Update and distribute the worksite model to all relevant modules within 1.0 s latency.
FR3: Ensure data integrity and consistency during update and distribution.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link meets latency and reliability requirements.
NGHE Solution has sufficient processing capacity.
open_risks: Network congestion or failure impacting update latency.
Single point of failure in NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational load.
